ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "churn" INTEGER /* Indicates if the customer has churned (1) or not (0) */,
  "account_weeks" INTEGER /* Number of weeks the customer has had the account */,
  "contract_renewal" INTEGER /* Indicates if the customer has renewed their contract */,
  "data_plan" INTEGER /* Indicates if the customer has a data plan */,
  "plan_type" TEXT /* Type of data plan (e.g., individual, family, business) */,
  "plan_expiration_date" TEXT /* Date when the data plan expires */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method used by the customer to pay their bills (e.g., credit card, bank transfer) */,
  "billing_address" TEXT /* Address where the customer receives their bills */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "usage_statistics" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer associated with the usage data */,
  "data_usage" REAL /* Amount of data usage in GB */,
  "day_mins" REAL /* Total minutes used during the day */,
  "day_calls" INTEGER /* Total number of calls made during the day */,
  "night_mins" REAL /* Total minutes used at night */,
  "night_calls" INTEGER /* Total number of calls made at night */,
  "roam_data_usage" REAL /* Amount of data usage while roaming in GB */,
  "roam_mins" REAL /* Total minutes spent roaming */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_usage_statistics_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "billing" (
  "billing_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each billing record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer associated with this billing record */,
  "monthly_charge" REAL /* Monthly charge for the customer */,
  "overage_fee" REAL /* Fee incurred for overage usage */,
  "roam_mins" REAL /* Minutes spent on roaming */,
  "late_fee" REAL /* Fee incurred for late payment */,
  "payment_date" TEXT /* Date when the bill was paid */,
  "payment_status" TEXT /* Status of the payment (e.g., paid, pending, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("billing_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_billing_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contract_renewals" (
  "renewal_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each renewal record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer associated with the renewal */,
  "renewal_status" INTEGER /* Indicates if the contract is renewed (1) or not (0) */,
  "renewal_date" TEXT /* Date when the contract was renewed */,
  "contract_type" TEXT /* Type of contract (e.g., 1-year, 2-year, month-to-month) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("renewal_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contract_renewals_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_service_calls" (
  "call_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service call record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who made the calls */,
  "num_calls" INTEGER /* Number of calls made by the customer to customer service */,
  "call_date" TEXT /* Date when the call was made */,
  "call_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the customer service call (e.g., billing issue, technical issue) */,
  "resolution_status" TEXT /* Status of the resolution (e.g., resolved, pending, escalated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("call_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_service_calls_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segmentation record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer in this segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer segment (e.g., High Usage, Low Usage) */,
  "segment_description" TEXT /* Description of the customer segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_segments_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictive_model" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer being analyzed */,
  "churn_probability" REAL /* Predicted probability of churn for the customer */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of predictive model used (e.g., logistic regression, decision tree) */,
  "model_date" TEXT /* Date when the model was run */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictive_model_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer providing feedback */,
  "satisfaction_rating" REAL /* Rating given by the customer for services received */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments provided by the customer regarding their experience */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date when the feedback was provided */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_feedback_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "promotional_offers" (
  "offer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each promotional offer */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who received the offer */,
  "offer_description" TEXT /* Description of the promotional offer */,
  "status" INTEGER /* Indicates if the offer was accepted (1) or not (0) */,
  "offer_date" TEXT /* Date when the offer was sent */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Date when the offer expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("offer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_promotional_offers_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who owns the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., smartphone, tablet, smartwatch) */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "operating_system" TEXT /* Operating system of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_devices_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_quality" (
  "network_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who experienced the network quality */,
  "signal_strength" REAL /* Strength of the signal (e.g., -50 dBm, -100 dBm) */,
  "data_speed" REAL /* Speed of the data connection (e.g., 10 Mbps, 100 Mbps) */,
  "network_type" TEXT /* Type of network (e.g., 4G, 5G, Wi-Fi) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("network_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_quality_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)
